decisions.summary: SHIP_DISPATCH_STALENESS_MS reduced from 2 min to 30 s; SHIP_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_MS reduced from 5 min to 90 s. Values match issue body verbatim.

Per-issue re-dispatch counter implemented as a module-level Map<string, number> with process-lifetime persistence (mirrors existing shipDispatchCooldowns lifecycle). NOT cleared on issue completion — cross-cycle signal is the whole point of the metric.
Log format is exact: `[deacon] Ship re-dispatched (N total for issue X)` — matches the issue body so existing operator grep/dashboard queries built against this string continue to work.

New 60s-staleness assertion uses vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.setSystemTime() with a fresh issueId, per the project's fake-timers-for-retry-tests rule. Existing tests in the file (STALE_TS = now - 10 min) are left unchanged — they still exercise the same behavior under the new 30 s threshold.
JSDoc comment block above checkUndispatchedShip is updated in the same bead as the constants — leaving doc-comment values stale after the constants change would be a small but real bandaid.

Patrol cadence is NOT touched. The deacon already runs frequently enough; with staleness=30s an eligible issue gets dispatched on the next tick, well inside the 60s target. Tuning patrol interval would be over-scoped.
Bead 1 (constants) blocks Bead 3 (60s test) — the test would fail against the old 2-min staleness. Bead 2 (counter+log) is independent of the other two.

hazards.summary: Module-level shipDispatchCooldowns and the new shipDispatchCounts maps persist across tests in the same file. Existing tests already hit this (PAN-977 vs PAN-888 trick); the new fake-timer test must use yet another fresh issueId.
hazards.mitigation: Use a distinct issueId (e.g. PAN-991 or PAN-1414-T1) in the new test. Do NOT attempt to reset the maps via importing internals — the existing tests document this constraint as the intended pattern.

hazards.summary: Lower cooldown (90 s) means more frequent re-dispatch attempts if the reactive chain is genuinely broken on an issue. activeRoleRunExists is the actual idempotency guard, so this is safe, but log volume on a misbehaving issue will increase.
hazards.mitigation: Acceptable by design — the per-issue counter is precisely the signal we want to surface; chattier logs on a broken issue are the bug we're trying to detect, not noise.

hazards.summary: Tightened staleness (30 s) could theoretically race the reactive scheduler if onIssueStateChange('shipping') takes >30 s to dispatch. The reactive path is described as firing within ~1 s.
hazards.mitigation: activeRoleRunExists protects against double-dispatch when a run is already in flight, and onIssueStateChange's own zombie-detection handles stale sessions. Even if the patrol fires a duplicate, it's a no-op.

hazards.summary: Cloister patrol cadence may not align perfectly with the 60s test target — if the patrol runs every N seconds and N > 30, an issue could become eligible at t=30 but not get patrolled until t=30+N.
hazards.mitigation: Out of scope per D6. The acceptance criterion is `patrol fires within 60 s of stale ship-eligible status`; the test exercises checkUndispatchedShip directly (skipping the patrol scheduler), which validates the function-level guarantee. If end-to-end cadence becomes a concern later, that is a separate follow-up issue.

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session started for PAN-1414
Auto-planning complete: 3 beads decomposed (constants, counter+log, fake-timer test). All inferred choices recorded in autoDecisions. No contradictions surfaced.
